comments.body:
No actionable comments were generated in the recent review. 🎉
ℹ️ Recent review info
⚙️ Run configuration **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro Plus **Run ID**: `6ddb60e2-a6ab-431b-a151-d42268cbf8cb`
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between cb9f7d1c0aa909fb9562455af40e99826e5814d7 and e40dbe16743f72ceb639fc104e2aae19718a6cdf.
📒 Files selected for processing (2) * `src/agentic_portfolio/web/runs.py` * `tests/test_runs.py`
---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Introduces single-flight run claiming and an environment-driven run cooldown. Adds `RunTracker.try_start(source, cooldown_seconds)`, integrates it into POST `/api/run` and the cron scheduler, documents `AGENTIC_PORTFOLIO_RUN_COOLDOWN_SECONDS` in `.env.example`, and expands tests for concurrency and cooldown behavior. ## Changes **Run Cooldown & Single-Flight Gating** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Configuration**
`.env.example`|Documents `AGENTIC_PORTFOLIO_RUN_COOLDOWN_SECONDS` with explanatory comments and default `1800`.| |**Data Shape / Validation**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/runs.py`|`RunTracker.current()` tightened to return `dict[str, Any] | None` by validating stored JSON before returning.| |**Core Single-Flight Logic**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/runs.py`|Adds `RunTracker.try_start(*, source: str, cooldown_seconds: float = 0.0) -> tuple[bool, str | None]` that atomically claims a run under the instance lock, returns `(False, "already_running")` if a run is active, enforces cooldown based on `completed_at` returning `(False, "cooldown")` if within window, and writes a new running state on success.| |**Helper & Environment Access**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/api.py`|Adds `os` import and `_run_cooldown_seconds()` that reads and parses `AGENTIC_PORTFOLIO_RUN_COOLDOWN_SECONDS` (defensive parsing, non-negative clamp, default `0.0`).| |**API Integration**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/api.py`|POST `/api/run` computes `source`, calls `tracker.try_start(source=..., cooldown_seconds=...)`, returns HTTP `409` when `"already_running"`, `429` when `"cooldown"`, and on success calls `_launch_run_in_background(source=..., ...)`. Removes redundant `tracker.mark_started` inside `_execute_run`.| |**Scheduler Integration**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/scheduler.py`|`_scheduled_run_callback` now calls `tracker.try_start(source="cron", cooldown_seconds=0.0)` and skips execution with a logged warning if claim fails; otherwise calls `_execute_run` with the claiming tracker.| |**RunTracker Tests**
`tests/test_runs.py`|Adds `TestTryStart` suite covering idle acquire, rejection when running, completed-with/without cooldown, cooldown expiry, zero-cooldown bypass, failed-state cooldown, and concurrent callers ensuring single winner.| |**API Endpoint Tests**
`tests/test_web_api.py`|Adds tests for POST `/api/run`: `409` when already running, `429` when within cooldown (env-configured), and `202` when cooldown disabled or elapsed; monkeypatches launcher and tracker to assert behavior.| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant API as POST /api/run participant Tracker as RunTracker participant Launcher as Background Launcher Client->>API: Request run API->>Tracker: try_start(source, cooldown_seconds) Tracker->>Tracker: Check status and completed_at alt Allowed Tracker->>Tracker: Write running state (started_at, source) Tracker-->>API: (True, None) API->>Launcher: _launch_run_in_background(source, ...) API-->>Client: 202 Accepted else Already running Tracker-->>API: (False, "already_running") API-->>Client: 409 Conflict else Cooldown Tracker-->>API: (False, "cooldown") API-->>Client: 429 Too Many Requests end ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~28 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 I hopped to claim the single slot, > > Locks snug, no two may trot. > > Cooldown keeps the echoes tame, > > Cron and manual play the same. > > A quiet thump — one run, one name.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 12.50% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | ✅ Passed | Title clearly and specifically describes the main changes: adding single-flight and cooldown gating mechanisms to the POST /api/run endpoint as a security hardening feature. | | Description check | ✅ Passed | Description is comprehensive, well-structured, and covers all template sections: detailed summary with context, explicit type marking, root cause analysis, multi-layered fix explanation, specific file changes, testing results, and deployment notes. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feat/run-singleflight-cooldown`

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@src/agentic_portfolio/web/runs.py`:
- Around line 80-84: The subtraction can raise TypeError when completed_at (from
datetime.fromisoformat) is naive; update the code around completed_at and the
elapsed calculation to normalize naive datetimes to UTC before subtraction:
after parsing completed_at (in the block that currently sets completed_at =
datetime.fromisoformat(completed_at_iso) and handles ValueError), check if
completed_at.tzinfo is None and, if so, set completed_at =
completed_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) (or otherwise convert to an aware UTC
datetime) so that the subsequent elapsed = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) -
completed_at).total_seconds() always subtracts two aware datetimes; this keeps
compatibility with legacy naive timestamps while preserving the existing
behavior of mark_completed and mark_failed which store aware ISO strings.
```

</details>

prior/status in try_start to handle the fallback empty dict case and optionally
log or sanitize unexpected shapes from self.current().
